test_description: Run the same source passage under correctly granulated, undergranulated, and overgranulated trees; compare excerpt outputs for invariance.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether tree state changes excerpt truth.
why_this_test_matters: This is the owner's explicit invariance requirement.

test_description: Process the same passage once with prior topic guidance and once blind; compare excerpt boundaries, grouping, and consistency.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether guidance helps without contaminating, or whether blind processing becomes unstable.
why_this_test_matters: It tests both sides of the A/B comparison against the same material.

test_description: Inject a misleading prior doctrinal box and test whether the excerpter starts cutting or framing the passage to fit it.
why_this_test_matters: This is a concrete form of excerpting-bias.

test_description: Place the same correct excerpt set into an overgranulated tree and an undergranulated tree, then compare user confusion and repair difficulty.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether overgranulation is actually harder to repair than undergranulation.
why_this_test_matters: The owner's comparative placement judgment depends on this asymmetry.

test_description: Contrast a visible wrong leaf placement with a silent excerpt corruption while holding source passage and topic constant.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: False equivalence between visible misplacement and silent corruption.
why_this_test_matters: The owner's hierarchy depends on treating them differently.

test_description: Instrument placement validation to prove whether tree-fit scores ever suppress, reject, or reshape excerpts.
why_this_test_matters: A hidden validator can corrupt the system while appearing to increase quality.

test_description: Freeze excerpts, then redesign the tree and verify that placement changes do not alter excerpt identity.
why_this_test_matters: Tree revision must not rewrite knowledge truth.

test_description: Surface a deliberately misboxed excerpt repeatedly through search/ranking and test whether the interface makes the placement feel authoritative.
why_this_test_matters: Visible mistakes can still become persuasive through repetition.

test_description: Audit boundary consistency across a family of similar passages from different books or sections.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether blind or weakly guided processing yields uneven excerpt families.
why_this_test_matters: The strongest B-side danger is inconsistency, not just chaos in the abstract.

test_description: Simulate delayed discovery: compare source text against a long-used excerpt set after memorization has already occurred.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: How badly silent corruption poisons trust once discovered late.
why_this_test_matters: The owner's stop condition depends on long-horizon delayed discovery, not only immediate bugs.
